data.title: REG - National Grid PLC - Director Declaration
data.description:
RNS Number : 6858A National Grid PLC 24 September 2025  

24 September 2025

National Grid plc ('National Grid' or the 'Company')

Director Declaration

In accordance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.9R(2), National Grid announces that John Pettigrew, Chief Executive of the Company, will join the Board of BAE Systems plc as a Non-executive Director and member of its Audit and Risk Committee with effect from 23 February 2026.

As announced on 1 May 2025, John will retire from his role as Chief Executive of National Grid effective 16 November 2025.

Julian Baddeley

Group Company Secretary

data.title: ZymIQ signs distribution agreement for Thailand
data.description:
ZymIQ Technology AB has signed an exclusive distribution agreement for its animal health products with the Thai company Vet AgriTech CO Ltd.

This year, ZymIQ has begun the internationalization of its animal health product range. Earlier this year, launches took place in countries including Denmark for private pet owners under the brand Kalzyme™, and in Hungary under the brand CombatClean™ K9 for military dogs. The next step in this process is now being taken with the signing of an exclusive distribution agreement for the first Asian country, Thailand, with Vet AgriTech for Kalzyme™. The agreement follows extensive testing and evaluations over recent years conducted through veterinarians and pharmacies in the Southeast Asian market.

Kalzyme™, already an established brand in the Swedish market, offers a range of enzyme-based dental and dermal products with clinically proven effects, developed by researchers and veterinarians to offer safe and effective health products that promote good health in dogs and cats.

Vet AgriTech operates in the manufacturing and distribution of pharmaceuticals and products for animals, covering both livestock and pets. The company was founded in 1995, has a turnover of approximately 1.9 billion THB (approx. 570 MSEK), and employs over 300 people. The company already represents other major international brands in animal health in Thailand.

Kong Nontapatamadul, CEO of Vet AgriTech CO Ltd, comments: “We find that ZymIQ works very professionally with its innovative products, and together we will bring great value to pet owners in Thailand going forward.”

“Securing an agreement with a well-established company in animal health in a country with over 70 million people, where more than 40% of households own a dog and cat, feels extremely positive. Our new agreement with Vet AgriTech is clear evidence that our product portfolio is highly attractive both to the veterinary profession and to distributors outside the Nordic region,” says Michael Edelborg Christensen, CEO of ZymIQ.

* Every care has been taken in the translation of this Press release. In the event of discrepancies, the Swedish original will supersede the English translation.

data.title: Belgian court overrules Alstom's appeal of SNCB's pick for 1.7 billion euro trains deal
data.description:
By Alban Kacher

Belgium's top administrative court on Wednesday rejected Alstom's request to suspend national rail operator SNCB's decision to name CAF as the preferred supplier for its AM30 train contract.

"The Council of State estimates, after an interim review, that none of the criticisms made by the company challenging the legality of this appointment decision were serious," it said in a statement.

In April, the court suspended the order following Alstom's appeal against the decision.

The order plays a crucial role in determining the future of Alstom's Bruges facility, which faces the threat of restructuring and possible closure.

SNCB's tender, the biggest the company has placed, consists of a first batch valued at 1.7 billion euros ($2 billion), with optional batches potentially bringing the total contract value to 5 billion euros.

Earlier this month, the Council rejected Siemens' separate appeal against SNCB's decision.

($1 = 0.8518 euros)

data.title: Lithium stocks light up as focus shifts to data
data.description:
Key points:
  • Major U.S. stock indexes essentially unchanged
  • Energy tops sector gains, real estate falls the most
  • Euro STOXX 600 index down ~0.3%
  • Dollar jumps, gold near record high; crude climbs ~1.5%, bitcoin rises
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield ticks up to ~4.14%

LITHIUM STOCKS LIGHT UP AS FOCUS SHIFTS TO DATA

U.S. stocks hovered near the flatline on Wednesday, lacking a fresh catalyst after all three major indexes snapped multi-day winning streaks in the previous session. Investors are now looking ahead to a heavy slate of economic data in the back half of the week.

After Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reiterated caution over further rate cuts on Tuesday, focus will be on the GDP report, weekly jobless claims, consumer confidence, PCE data spread across Thursday and Friday.

U.S.-listed shares of Lithium Americas soared more than 90% after a report said the Trump administration is seeking up to a 10% stake in the miner. Lithium Americas confirmed it is in talks with the U.S. Department of Energy and General Motors , its partner in the Thacker Pass JV, regarding the first draw on the $2.26 billion loan, lifting GM shares by 2%.

Other lithium stocks rallied in sympathy: Albemarle jumped 5.4% to lead gains on the S&P 500 , while Sigma Lithium up 9.6%, U.S.-listed shares of SQM added 3.5%.

In contrast, Freeport-McMoRan slumped 9.5% after the miner forecast lower consolidated sales for copper and gold in the third quarter.

Alibaba ADRs jumped 7.7% after announcing a tie-up with Nvidia, global data center expansion plans and new artificial intelligence products. Other China stocks got a boost too.

Among S&P sectors, the energy index led gains and was trading near a six-month highs. Real estate dropped the most, down 0.4%.

data.title: Lithium Americas Shares Jump Amid Report of Possible U.S. Government Stake
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Lithium Americas shares soared Wednesday morning after a Reuters report said the U.S. government is in talks to take a stake as big as 10% in the Canadian mining company.

Shares trading in Toronto rose over 81% to 7.65 Canadian dollars ($5.53).

The move by the Trump administration would be its latest effort to leverage loans made under the Biden administration into a stake in a company, like it did with tech giant Intel, as it looks to encourage U.S. manufacturing capacity.

Reuters reported the news late Tuesday.

Lithium Americas is developing a Nevada lithium mine called Thacker Pass along with General Motors, backed by a $2.26 billion loan from the U.S. Energy Department.

The company was awarded the loan under the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing program to help build out the processing facilities at Thacker Pass, which is among the largest known lithium deposits in the U.S.

But the project has come under scrutiny over its viability and U.S. administration officials have raised concerns about the company's ability to repay the loan given sluggish lithium prices.

Earlier on Wednesday, Lithium Americas said it was in talks with the Department of Energy and its partner General Motors over the terms of its $2.26 billion government loan for the project.

The talks are meant to cover requirements that must be met before the financing can be used, details of the loan itself, as well as new requests from the DOE that could add conditions or alter its terms, the Canadian miner said. GM, a partner in the project, is participating in the discussions.

Lithium is a critical mineral used in electric vehicle batteries, smartphones and renewable energy storage. In recent years it has become a priority for the U.S. as it seeks to cut reliance on foreign suppliers.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com
